Florida · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology under the Florida Department of Health (DOH). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$180
$100 application + $80 initial license
Renewal
$80
Every 2 years (December 31, odd years)
Processing Time
4 to 8 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

Mailing Address

4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C-06, Tallahassee, FL 32399-3256

Physical Address

4052 Bald Cypress Way, Tallahassee, FL 32399

Phone & Fax

Phone: (850) 245-4444

Fax: (850) 921-6184

Email

MQA.SpeechLanguagePathology@flhealth.gov

Credential Categories

Florida SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Florida. Requires a master's degree, nine months of supervised professional employment, and a passing Praxis score.

Provisional SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates who have not yet passed the Praxis exam. Allows supervised practice for up to one year while completing remaining requirements.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Florida Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's or doctoral degree with a major emphasis in speech-language pathology from a CAA-accredited program. Must include at least 300 clock hours of supervised clinical practicum (200+ hours in SLP).

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331). The current passing score is 162. Proof of passing must be dated within three years of application.

Complete Supervised Professional Employment

Complete nine months of supervised professional employment experience. If the Praxis has not been passed, a provisional license must be obtained first to begin supervised practice in Florida.

Submit Application via MQA Portal

Apply online through the Florida MQA Services Portal. Pay the $100 application fee and $80 initial license fee. Submit official transcripts, Praxis scores, and complete electronic fingerprinting/background screening.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsSent directly from the degree-granting institution to the Board office.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331. Must be dated within three years of application.
  • Supervised employment verificationDocumentation of nine months of supervised professional employment experience.
  • Electronic fingerprinting/background screeningRequired as of July 1, 2025 (HB 975). Both initial applicants and renewals must comply.
Telepractice

Florida Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Florida permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Florida SLP license to provide services to patients located in Florida. Telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person services. Florida does not currently participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Florida issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates who have not yet passed the Praxis exam. This allows supervised practice for up to one year. The provisional licensee must work under the direct supervision of a fully licensed SLP in Florida.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Florida does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Florida license and meet all state-specific requirements. However, applicants holding the ASHA CCC-SLP may have some documentation requirements streamlined. Florida does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

Frequently Asked Questions

Florida SLP FAQ

How much does a Florida SLP license cost?

The total initial licensure cost is approximately $180, which includes a $100 nonrefundable application fee and an $80 initial license fee. Biennial renewal is $80 if renewed before expiration.

What are the new fingerprinting requirements for Florida SLPs?

Effective July 1, 2025, all healthcare professionals in Florida, including SLPs, must complete electronic fingerprinting and background screening for both initial licensure and renewal. This applies to HB 975 passed during the 2024 legislative session.

Can I practice SLP in Florida with a provisional license?

Yes. Florida issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates who have not yet passed the Praxis exam. This allows supervised practice for up to one year while you complete remaining licensure requirements.
